description: Manifest of review shards for /review skill. See design/reviews/README.md for context.

shards.name: certs
shards.title: Certificate / proxy path
shards.notes: Cross-platform cert handling, MITM detection, multi-tool trust store coordination. Highest blast radius - a regression here breaks every HTTPS-dependent tool on a corporate network.

shards.name: orchestration
shards.title: Setup orchestration (nix/setup.sh + phase library)
shards.notes: Phase-based orchestrator + side-effect stubs. Touches every install/upgrade run. Idempotency, error propagation, and phase-boundary contracts are load-bearing.

shards.name: nx-cli
shards.title: nx CLI surface (sourced into user shells)
shards.notes: Sourced into every interactive bash/zsh on every login. Bash 3.2 compat, zsh-compat, and shell-state hygiene (no leaked variables, no surprising aliases) are critical.

shards.name: config-templates
shards.title: Shell / PowerShell / oh-my-posh config templates
shards.notes: Templates rendered into user profiles via managed blocks. Cross-shell parity (bash/zsh/pwsh) and idempotent rendering are the main concerns.

shards.name: system-installers
shards.title: System-scope installers (root-required)
shards.notes: Run as root, per-distro arms (alpine/arch/fedora/debian/ubuntu/opensuse). Mistakes here can brick a system or leak privileges.

shards.name: wsl-orchestration
shards.title: WSL orchestration (Windows host)
shards.notes: Runs on the Windows host before any WSL distro exists. Encoding, path translation, distro lifecycle. No CI coverage - only mocked Pester tests, so review must take the place of integration tests.

shards.name: precommit-hooks
shards.title: Pre-commit hook scripts
shards.notes: Hooks gate every commit. False positives slow down work; false negatives let regressions through. Detection logic and exit codes need the same scrutiny as production code.

shards.name: test-quality
shards.title: Test suites (bats + Pester)
shards.notes: Reviewing the tests themselves: are they testing the right thing, are mocks too lenient, do test names lie about what they assert. Low blast radius (test code), but compounds: a bad test misleads every future reader.

shards.name: enterprise-readiness
shards.title: Enterprise readiness posture
shards.notes: Cross-cutting posture lens (not code-correctness). Two binding constraints: (a) standalone integrity must hold, (b) no pollution by unused enterprise scaffolding. Findings have two output paths: code/doc fixes here, OR additions to enterprise.md as integration-side responsibility. The resist-adding-code path is the load-bearing mechanism.
